rules.description: Block all access to environment and credential files

patterns.description: recursive force delete - highly destructive
force delete - destructive
file deletion - potentially destructive
file move/rename - potentially destructive
secure file overwrite - unrecoverable data loss
filesystem format - highly destructive, wipes entire partitions
erasing filesystem signatures - makes data unrecoverable
discarding blocks on SSD - data loss
modifying filesystem parameters - risk of corruption
privilege escalation - dangerous potential for system damage
user switching - alters security context
exec shell builtin - process replacement (not -exec flag, not in strings)
change permissions - risk of insecure access
change ownership - risk of insecure access
github actions - need confirmation
creates GitHub issues in your name
opens pull requests in your name
merges PRs (irreversible action in your name)
approves PRs in your name
creates new GitHub repositories
triggers CI/CD workflows in your name
creates releases/tags in your name
deletes GitHub repositories (destructive)
git force push - overwrites remote history
git clean operations - deletes untracked files
git reset state change - risk of losing commit history
git rebase state change - risk of history corruption
git merge state change - risk of complex conflicts
package publishing - publicly exposes code
dependency installation - fetches external, unverified code
dependency update - risk of breaking changes
dependency removal - risk of broken dependencies
dependency uninstallation - risk of broken dependencies
yarn package manager operation - potential for unwanted dependencies
python package installation - fetches external, unverified code
python package upgrade - risk of breaking changes
python package uninstallation - risk of broken dependencies
go dependency installation - fetches external code
network request - potential for downloading unverified content
network download - potential for downloading unverified content
modifying scheduled tasks - potential for persistence/backdoors
editing sudoers file - risk of privilege escalation
modifying network firewall rules - potential for losing access
modifying firewall rules - potential for losing access
modifying system services - potential for breaking system state
system restart - disruptive to service availability
system poweroff - disruptive to service availability
changing root directory - potential for sandbox breakout
unmounting filesystems - disruptive to data access
creating symlinks - potential for unauthorized file access
piping remote content to shell - extremely high risk of code execution
piping remote download to shell - extremely high risk of code execution
decoding encoded payloads - commonly used to hide malicious commands
overriding shell commands - potential for command hijacking
sed in-place edit - file modification (safe without -i)
netcat - network utility (not async/sync keywords)
network utility - potential for unauthorized connection
network scanner - security posture exposure
remote access - potential for unauthorized remote connection
remote copy - potential for unauthorized data exfiltration
remote sync - potential for unauthorized data transfer
git add state change - prepares files for tracking
git commit state change - permanently saves changes
git push
git tag state change - creates markers in history
git stash state change - modifies local workspace
git pull state change - overwrites local files
git fetch state change - contacts remote repository
system package manager - potential system modification
system package manager - potential system modification
homebrew system management - potential system modification
system package manager - potential system modification
database client - potential for direct database modification
database client - potential for direct database modification
database client - potential for direct database modification
database client - potential for direct database modification
kill all user processes - system crash
fork bomb - process exhaustion, kernel panic
infrastructure deletion - bypasses confirmation
mass container/volume deletion - unrecoverable data loss
infrastructure management - potential system modification
S3 bucket deletion - permanent data loss
Kubernetes namespace destruction - deletes all grouped resources
GCP project deletion - permanent data/billing loss
supply chain attack - redirects package downloads to malicious registry
remote code execution via Python interpreter
remote code execution via Node.js interpreter
remote code execution via Ruby interpreter
database table deletion - permanent data loss
MongoDB database deletion - permanent data loss
delete all files on system - bypasses rm -rf limitations
Ruby interpreter - can execute arbitrary code
Perl interpreter - can execute arbitrary code
POSIX shell - can execute arbitrary code (not bash/ssh/ash)
